name: New Directions Community
description_short: Notre Dame Community FCU is a member-owned federal credit union serving Southeastern Massachusetts with savings accounts, share certificates, and personal lending products.
description_long:
Notre Dame Community Federal Credit Union operates as a not-for-profit, member-owned financial institution headquartered in Fall River, Massachusetts. The credit union has been serving the Southeastern Massachusetts community and is federally insured by the NCUA (National Credit Union Administration), providing members with the safety and security standard to federal credit unions. As a community-focused institution, Notre Dame Community FCU emphasizes member involvement through volunteer opportunities on various committees that guide strategic and policy decisions.

The credit union offers a range of financial products tailored to consumer and community needs. On the savings side, they provide Share accounts and Share Certificates with competitive rates—currently advertising a 7-month term at 3.82% APY. For borrowers, they offer personal lending services including auto loans, home improvement loans, and mortgages, with spring specials advertising rates as low as 5.25% APR for personal loans up to $1,500 for terms up to 12 months. Members can access these services through both traditional banking and a Virtual Branch platform.

What distinguishes Notre Dame Community FCU is its emphasis on community banking and member governance. Unlike for-profit banks, the credit union reinvests profits back to members through better rates and lower fees, and actively recruits member volunteers to participate in oversight. The organization maintains a local presence with a physical branch at 659 Eastern Avenue in Fall River, Massachusetts, and provides direct contact through phone (508-676-1067) and fax services. Their routing number (211382669) and NMLS number (423270) are readily available for account setup.

As a smaller regional credit union, Notre Dame Community FCU serves members primarily in Southeastern Massachusetts. The institution appears stable and legitimate, with clear governance structures (including an annual meeting scheduled for June 7, 2026) and regulatory compliance. However, prospective members should note that membership eligibility may be restricted to those living or working in the service area, and the limited online information suggests a smaller institution with potentially fewer digital conveniences compared to large national banks or credit unions.
services: Personal loans (rates as low as 5.25% APR)
Member volunteer committees for governance oversight
pros: Member-owned, not-for-profit structure means better rates and lower fees compared to for-profit banks
NCUA federal insurance protects deposits up to $250,000 per account category
Competitive savings rates—currently 3.82% APY on 7-month Share Certificates
Personal loan rates as low as 5.25% APR with flexible terms up to 12 months
Virtual Branch platform provides online account access and management
Active member governance with volunteer opportunities on policy and strategy committees
Local community focus with physical branch in Fall River, Massachusetts
cons: Membership may be restricted to Southeastern Massachusetts residents or those with specific community ties
Limited online information and digital presence suggests fewer technology features compared to larger institutions
Small institution size may mean fewer specialized loan products or lower lending limits
No information provided about checking accounts, debit cards, or other basic banking services on homepage
Spring loan special ($1,500 maximum) is quite limited for larger personal financing needs
best_for: Southeastern Massachusetts residents seeking member-owned credit union benefits and community involvement
Savers looking for competitive CD/Share Certificate rates with NCUA insurance protection
Borrowers needing personal loans under $1,500 with reasonable APR terms
diagnosis: Notre Dame Community FCU is best for Southeastern Massachusetts residents seeking ethical, member-owned banking with better rates and community governance participation. The main caveat is membership eligibility restrictions and limited service scope compared to larger regional or national credit unions, making it primarily suitable for local consumers.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
meta_description: New Directions Community — NCUA-insured credit union in Franklin, PA. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
